file.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Arch Linux Package Installer with Logging
# -----------------------------------------
# This script installs a list of packages via pacman and yay (for AUR)
# Logs failed installations to failed_packages.log

# File to log failed packages
LOG_FILE="failed_packages.log"
: > "$LOG_FILE"  # Clear previous log

# List of packages
PACKAGES=(
    git curl mercurial make binutils gcc bison wmctrl util-linux zlib
    shellcheck shfmt awesome rofi picom xclip flameshot xdotool fish
    eza btop atuin less bat fzf zoxide neovim ripgrep unzip go
    lazygit lxappearance xorg-xinit xorg-server kitty openssh xorg-setxkbmap
    noto-fonts-emoji nerd-fonts ttf-liberation ttf-dejavu alsa-utils
    playerctl pipewire pipewire-alsa pipewire-pulse pipewire-jack
    nvidia-dkms linux-headers nvidia-utils nvidia-settings xorg-xrandr rbenv ruby-build libyaml
    nvm fisher difftastic lazydocker
)

# Packages that are likely AUR-only
AUR_PACKAGES=("brave-browser" "nerd-fonts" "light" "bsdmainutils" "i3lock-fancy")

# Check if a package is AUR-only
is_aur() {
    for p in "${AUR_PACKAGES[@]}"; do [[ "$p" == "$1" ]] && return 0; done
    return 1
}

# Function to install packages via pacman
install_pacman_package() {
    local pkg="$1"
    if ! sudo pacman -S --noconfirm --needed "$pkg"; then
        echo "$pkg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "[FAILED] $pkg"
    else
        echo "[INSTALLED] $pkg"
    fi
}

# Function to install packages via yay (AUR)
install_aur_package() {
    local pkg="$1"
    if ! yay -S --noconfirm --needed "$pkg"; then
        echo "$pkg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "[FAILED] $pkg"
    else
        echo "[INSTALLED] $pkg"
    fi
}

# Main installation loop
for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if is_aur "$pkg"; then
        install_aur_package "$pkg"
    else
        install_pacman_package "$pkg"
    fi
done

echo "Installation complete!"
if [[ -s "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "Some packages failed to install. Check $LOG_FILE for details."
else
    echo "All packages installed successfully!"
fi

# Set fish as default shell
if command -v fish &>/dev/null; then
    fish_path=$(which fish)
    grep -qF "$fish_path" /etc/shells || echo "$fish_path" | sudo tee -a /etc/shells
    sudo usermod -s "$fish_path" "$(logname)"
    echo "[SET] fish as default shell"
fi

content.text:
Found the bug. The AUR packages (`brave-browser`, `light`, `bsdmainutils`, `i3lock-fancy`) are only in `AUR_PACKAGES` but **not** in the main `PACKAGES` loop. The loop only iterates over `PACKAGES`, so AUR packages are never processed — they're just checked against but never queued for installation.

The fix is to loop over `AUR_PACKAGES` separately after the main loop:

input.old_string:
# Main installation loop
for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if is_aur "$pkg"; then
        install_aur_package "$pkg"
    else
        install_pacman_package "$pkg"
    fi
done
input.new_string:
# Main installation loop
for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if is_aur "$pkg"; then
        install_aur_package "$pkg"
    else
        install_pacman_package "$pkg"
    fi
done

# Install AUR-only packages
for pkg in "${AUR_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    install_aur_package "$pkg"
done

done

# Install AUR-only packages

done

# Arch Linux Package Installer with Logging
# -----------------------------------------
# This script installs a list of packages via pacman and yay (for AUR)
# Logs failed installations to failed_packages.log

# File to log failed packages
LOG_FILE="failed_packages.log"

# List of packages

    git curl mercurial make binutils gcc bison wmctrl util-linux zlib
    shellcheck shfmt awesome rofi picom xclip flameshot xdotool fish
    eza btop atuin less bat fzf zoxide neovim ripgrep unzip go
    lazygit lxappearance xorg-xinit xorg-server kitty openssh xorg-setxkbmap
    noto-fonts-emoji nerd-fonts ttf-liberation ttf-dejavu alsa-utils
    playerctl pipewire pipewire-alsa pipewire-pulse pipewire-jack
    nvidia-dkms linux-headers nvidia-utils nvidia-settings xorg-xrandr rbenv ruby-build libyaml
    nvm fisher difftastic lazydocker
)

# Packages that are likely AUR-only
AUR_PACKAGES=("brave-browser" "nerd-fonts" "light" "bsdmainutils" "i3lock-fancy")

# Check if a package is AUR-only
is_aur() {
    for p in "${AUR_PACKAGES[@]}"; do [[ "$p" == "$1" ]] && return 0; done
    return 1
}

# Function to install packages via pacman
install_pacman_package() {
    local pkg="$1"
    if ! sudo pacman -S --noconfirm --needed "$pkg"; then
        echo "$pkg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "[FAILED] $pkg"
    else

    fi
}

    local pkg="$1"
    if ! yay -S --noconfirm --needed "$pkg"; then
        echo "$pkg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "[FAILED] $pkg"
    else
        echo "[INSTALLED] $pkg"
    fi
}

# Main installation loop
for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if is_aur "$pkg"; then
        install_aur_package "$pkg"
    else
        install_pacman_package "$pkg"
    fi
done

echo "Installation complete!"
if [[ -s "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "Some packages failed to install. Check $LOG_FILE for details."
else
    echo "All packages installed successfully!"
fi

# Set fish as default shell
if command -v fish &>/dev/null; then
    fish_path=$(which fish)
    grep -qF "$fish_path" /etc/shells || echo "$fish_path" | sudo tee -a /etc/shells
    sudo usermod -s "$fish_path" "$(logname)"
    echo "[SET] fish as default shell"
fi

content.text: Also worth noting: `nerd-fonts` appears in both `PACKAGES` and `AUR_PACKAGES`. If it's an AUR package, remove it from `PACKAGES`; if it's in the official repos, remove it from `AUR_PACKAGES`. Do you want me to clean that up too?

message.content:
why in this loop there is AUR if 

for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if is_aur "$pkg"; then
        install_aur_package "$pkg"
    else
        install_pacman_package "$pkg"
    fi

content.thinking:
The user is asking why the `is_aur` check exists in the main loop when none of the packages in `PACKAGES` are in `AUR_PACKAGES` anymore (and originally, only `nerd-fonts` was in both, which we just removed from AUR_PACKAGES).

Since we now have a separate loop for AUR packages, the `is_aur` check in the main loop is dead code — no package in `PACKAGES` is in `AUR_PACKAGES`, so `is_aur` will always return 1 (false) for every package in `PACKAGES`.

The user is essentially pointing out this is redundant/dead code now. We can simplify the loop to just call `install_pacman_package` for everything in `PACKAGES`.

content.text: Good catch — it's dead code now. Since `PACKAGES` contains only pacman packages and `AUR_PACKAGES` is looped separately, `is_aur` will always return false inside that loop. Simplifying it:

input.old_string:
for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if is_aur "$pkg"; then
        install_aur_package "$pkg"
    else
        install_pacman_package "$pkg"
    fi
done
input.new_string:
for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    install_pacman_package "$pkg"
done

#!/bin/bash

# Arch Linux Package Installer with Logging
# -----------------------------------------
# This script installs a list of packages via pacman and yay (for AUR)
# Logs failed installations to failed_packages.log

# File to log failed packages
LOG_FILE="failed_packages.log"
: > "$LOG_FILE"  # Clear previous log

# List of packages
PACKAGES=(
    git curl mercurial make binutils gcc bison wmctrl util-linux zlib
    shellcheck shfmt awesome rofi picom xclip flameshot xdotool fish
    eza btop atuin less bat fzf zoxide neovim ripgrep unzip go
    lazygit lxappearance xorg-xinit xorg-server kitty openssh xorg-setxkbmap
    noto-fonts-emoji nerd-fonts ttf-liberation ttf-dejavu alsa-utils
    playerctl pipewire pipewire-alsa pipewire-pulse pipewire-jack
    nvidia-dkms linux-headers nvidia-utils nvidia-settings xorg-xrandr rbenv ruby-build libyaml
    nvm fisher difftastic lazydocker
)

# Packages that are likely AUR-only
AUR_PACKAGES=("brave-browser" "light" "bsdmainutils" "i3lock-fancy")

# Check if a package is AUR-only
is_aur() {
    for p in "${AUR_PACKAGES[@]}"; do [[ "$p" == "$1" ]] && return 0; done
    return 1
}

# Function to install packages via pacman
install_pacman_package() {
    local pkg="$1"
    if ! sudo pacman -S --noconfirm --needed "$pkg"; then
        echo "$pkg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "[FAILED] $pkg"
    else
        echo "[INSTALLED] $pkg"
    fi
}

# Function to install packages via yay (AUR)
install_aur_package() {
    local pkg="$1"
    if ! yay -S --noconfirm --needed "$pkg"; then
        echo "$pkg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "[FAILED] $pkg"
    else
        echo "[INSTALLED] $pkg"
    fi
}

# Main installation loop
for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if is_aur "$pkg"; then
        install_aur_package "$pkg"
    else
        install_pacman_package "$pkg"
    fi
done

# Install AUR-only packages
for pkg in "${AUR_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    install_aur_package "$pkg"
done

echo "Installation complete!"
if [[ -s "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "Some packages failed to install. Check $LOG_FILE for details."
else
    echo "All packages installed successfully!"
fi

# Set fish as default shell
if command -v fish &>/dev/null; then
    fish_path=$(which fish)
    grep -qF "$fish_path" /etc/shells || echo "$fish_path" | sudo tee -a /etc/shells
    sudo usermod -s "$fish_path" "$(logname)"
    echo "[SET] fish as default shell"
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Arch Linux Package Installer with Logging
# -----------------------------------------
# This script installs a list of packages via pacman and yay (for AUR)
# Logs failed installations to failed_packages.log

# File to log failed packages
LOG_FILE="failed_packages.log"
: > "$LOG_FILE"  # Clear previous log

# List of packages
PACKAGES=(
    git curl mercurial make binutils gcc bison wmctrl util-linux zlib
    shellcheck shfmt awesome rofi picom xclip flameshot xdotool fish
    eza btop atuin less bat fzf zoxide neovim ripgrep unzip go
    lazygit lxappearance xorg-xinit xorg-server kitty openssh xorg-setxkbmap
    noto-fonts-emoji nerd-fonts ttf-liberation ttf-dejavu alsa-utils
    playerctl pipewire pipewire-alsa pipewire-pulse pipewire-jack
    nvidia-dkms linux-headers nvidia-utils nvidia-settings xorg-xrandr rbenv ruby-build libyaml
    nvm fisher difftastic lazydocker
)

# Packages that are likely AUR-only
AUR_PACKAGES=("brave-browser" "light" "bsdmainutils" "i3lock-fancy")

# Check if a package is AUR-only
is_aur() {
    for p in "${AUR_PACKAGES[@]}"; do [[ "$p" == "$1" ]] && return 0; done
    return 1
}

# Function to install packages via pacman
install_pacman_package() {
    local pkg="$1"
    if ! sudo pacman -S --noconfirm --needed "$pkg"; then
        echo "$pkg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "[FAILED] $pkg"
    else
        echo "[INSTALLED] $pkg"
    fi
}

# Function to install packages via yay (AUR)
install_aur_package() {
    local pkg="$1"
    if ! yay -S --noconfirm --needed "$pkg"; then
        echo "$pkg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "[FAILED] $pkg"
    else
        echo "[INSTALLED] $pkg"
    fi
}

# Main installation loop
for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    install_pacman_package "$pkg"
done

# Install AUR-only packages
for pkg in "${AUR_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    install_aur_package "$pkg"
done

echo "Installation complete!"
if [[ -s "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "Some packages failed to install. Check $LOG_FILE for details."
else
    echo "All packages installed successfully!"
fi

# Set fish as default shell
if command -v fish &>/dev/null; then
    fish_path=$(which fish)
    grep -qF "$fish_path" /etc/shells || echo "$fish_path" | sudo tee -a /etc/shells
    sudo usermod -s "$fish_path" "$(logname)"
    echo "[SET] fish as default shell"
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Arch Linux Package Installer with Logging
# -----------------------------------------
# This script installs a list of packages via pacman and yay (for AUR)
# Logs failed installations to failed_packages.log

# File to log failed packages
LOG_FILE="failed_packages.log"
: > "$LOG_FILE"  # Clear previous log

# List of packages
PACKAGES=(
    git curl mercurial make binutils gcc bison wmctrl util-linux zlib
    shellcheck shfmt awesome rofi picom xclip flameshot xdotool fish
    eza btop atuin less bat fzf zoxide neovim ripgrep unzip go
    lazygit lxappearance xorg-xinit xorg-server kitty openssh xorg-setxkbmap
    noto-fonts-emoji nerd-fonts ttf-liberation ttf-dejavu alsa-utils
    playerctl pipewire pipewire-alsa pipewire-pulse pipewire-jack
    nvidia-dkms linux-headers nvidia-utils nvidia-settings xorg-xrandr rbenv ruby-build libyaml
    nvm fisher difftastic lazydocker
)

# Packages that are likely AUR-only
AUR_PACKAGES=("brave-browser" "light" "bsdmainutils" "i3lock-fancy")

# Function to install packages via pacman
install_pacman_package() {
    local pkg="$1"
    if ! sudo pacman -S --noconfirm --needed "$pkg"; then
        echo "$pkg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "[FAILED] $pkg"
    else
        echo "[INSTALLED] $pkg"
    fi
}

# Function to install packages via yay (AUR)
install_aur_package() {
    local pkg="$1"
    if ! yay -S --noconfirm --needed "$pkg"; then
        echo "$pkg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "[FAILED] $pkg"
    else
        echo "[INSTALLED] $pkg"
    fi
}

# Main installation loop
for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    install_pacman_package "$pkg"
done

# Install AUR-only packages
for pkg in "${AUR_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    install_aur_package "$pkg"
done

echo "Installation complete!"
if [[ -s "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "Some packages failed to install. Check $LOG_FILE for details."
else
    echo "All packages installed successfully!"
fi

# Set fish as default shell
if command -v fish &>/dev/null; then
    fish_path=$(which fish)
    grep -qF "$fish_path" /etc/shells || echo "$fish_path" | sudo tee -a /etc/shells
    sudo usermod -s "$fish_path" "$(logname)"
    echo "[SET] fish as default shell"
fi

content.text:
A few issues:

1. **`nvm`, `fisher`, `lazydocker`** — these are not pacman packages. `nvm` and `fisher` are installed via their own install scripts, not pacman/yay. `lazydocker` is AUR. Move them out.
2. **`nerd-fonts`** — this is a font group in the AUR (`nerd-fonts` meta), not an official repo package. Should move to AUR.
3. **`rbenv` and `ruby-build`** — not in official Arch repos, they're AUR packages.
4. **`logname`** — unreliable in sudo contexts; `$SUDO_USER` or `$USER` is safer.
5. **`which fish`** — `command -v fish` is already used above and is more portable; use it consistently.
